title: Fix Candid interface mismatch for blog post creation

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Expected v3 response body' Candid interface mismatch error during blog post creation and editing
acceptanceCriteria: A logged-in user can submit the Create Blog Post form without receiving an 'Expected v3 response body' error.
The backend Candid interface for creating a blog post matches what the frontend sends.
The created post appears in the admin posts list after successful submission.
Editing an existing blog post also completes without a Candid mismatch error.

file_operations.description: Fix the Candid serialization of ExternalBlob instances when calling actor.createPost(). Ensure that the image parameter is properly constructed as ExternalBlob | null (not undefined or an invalid type). Ensure contentImages array contains only valid ExternalBlob instances. Verify that all parameters match the backend interface signature exactly, including proper handling of null vs undefined for optional fields like image, imageSize, and publishedAt.

Fix the Candid serialization of ExternalBlob instances when calling actor.updatePost(). Ensure that when replacing or keeping the existing image, the image parameter is properly constructed as ExternalBlob | null. Ensure contentImages array contains only valid ExternalBlob instances. Verify that all parameters match the backend interface signature exactly, including proper handling of null vs undefined for optional fields.

Review and fix the createPost and updatePost mutation functions to ensure they correctly handle ExternalBlob serialization before passing data to the backend actor. Ensure all parameters are properly typed and match the backend interface signature. Add proper null/undefined handling for optional parameters to prevent Candid deserialization errors.
